message: a non-trivial multi-step workflow just completed (>=6 tool calls). Two optional ways to learn from it: (1) if the PROCEDURE is REPEATABLE, capture it as a skill — `/learn how I just did this` (the /learn flow dedups (patch, don't duplicate) and gates the rationale through write-gate; the skill is born `proposed`, slash-only, won't auto-fire, until usage earns promotion); (2) if the TASK will recur or is worth a lesson, retrospect it — `/retro` (task-retrospective decides whether the lesson belongs in a wiki fact, a gate, a skill, or an always-on rule). Both advisory; skip if this was a one-off.
